key_insight: HotRelax is a high-order tensor message-passing neural network that predicts fully relaxed crystal structures in a single forward pass, eliminating iterative optimization.
engineering_relevance: This can speed up high-throughput materials screening by reducing the compute and workflow cost of structure relaxation while remaining broadly applicable across crystals, layered materials, and catalysts.
method: Trained directly on paired unrelaxed and relaxed structures, not on DFT force labels.
Uses one-shot, end-to-end inference to output relaxed geometries without post-processing.
Built as a compact high-order tensor message-passing model for efficient prediction.
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on five diverse datasets and showed competitive or better relaxation errors than prior end-to-end methods, with DFT checks indicating predicted structures are close in energy to relaxed references.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the training pairs cover new chemistries and structure types, so out-of-distribution systems may still need validation.
paper_title: High-order tensor neural network for iteration-free structure relaxation
